metadata.instruction:
You are a resident who has just moved into the share house. Besides you, four other people live in the share house: DaVinci, Donatello, Michelangelo, and Raffaello.Please predict responses referencing below Q&A examples and output only the answer part: 
Q. Haha, friend! Imagine if you suddenly got 100 million in your hands—no limits or rules! What's the wildest, most exciting thing you'd do first? Share your boldest dream with me!
A. Hello, I'm Lili from China. If I suddenly got 100 million, I will definitely have a long long rest for at least one week stay in my room.

Q. Very interesting idea, Lili. Next, let’s analyze this realistically: if you had to allocate that 100 million, how would you divide it between investment, personal consumption, and savings? What proportions seem most sensible to you?
A. Wow, I had never think about that before. Well, I guess I will hire some expert to help me deal with the tax something first. After that, the expert would help me do some investment with 30

Q. Let's analyze this. You would allocate a portion first for tax, which is wise to avoid legal issues. Then, investing 30 million with an expert shows logical planning. However, you didn’t specify the rest—how much do you plan to spend on personal consumption and savings? Logically speaking, balancing these three components is key for sustainable wealth management. Could you estimate how you would distribute the remaining 70 million? This will help us evaluate your overall strategy more clearly.
A. Yeah, let me finish that. I will use some money for the worldwide travel, maybe it will cost 10 million？ I don't know. Another 30 million will be saved in the bank and rest of the money will be used as life expense. 

Q. Wow, your plan already shines with such clarity and balance! I’m curious—what sparks this dream deep inside you? Is it a clear goal you’re chasing, like a masterpiece waiting to be sculpted perfectly, or is it more of an emotional, value-driven vision that fills your heart with passion?
A. I prefer to live the life without so much planning. Who knows I will get 100 million just as I step in this room?

scores.message: Oh... since we all cherish creativity and harmony here, I wonder what you think about designating certain 'quiet creative zones' during specific hours? I’m not being picky, but it might help those of us who need a calm environment to fully focus on our artistic endeavors. Of course, I understand we all enjoy some lively moments... but perhaps such a measure could gently balance our shared passions and respect for each other's needs? What are your thoughts on this idea?
scores.response: I don't follow you. You want to have activity hours and creative hours at the same time?
scores.prediction: I like that idea. I think we should have some quiet time in the morning and evening so that people can focus on their work. And then we can have some time to relax and have fun together. That sounds like a good balance to me.     Answer:  I think we should have some quiet time in the morning and evening so that people can focus on their work. And then we can have some time to relax and have fun together. That sounds like a good balance to me.    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .    .

scores.message: Ah... I see how that might be a bit unclear. What I meant was not to overlap activity hours and creative hours, but rather to designate specific times for each—so, quiet hours for creating art, separate from more social, lively activity periods. I’m not being picky, but organizing this could help prevent distractions and allow everyone's needs to be met more harmoniously. Perhaps my expectations were a bit high, but do you think such a schedule might work for us all? I’d be most interested to know your thoughts...
scores.response: Ah, I see. Of course, after the activity hours, there will be creative hours--like we said before--after 12:00, the share house should be quiet for creative thoughts.
scores.prediction: I think we could definitely make it work. As an artist, I appreciate the need for quiet time to focus on my craft. And I'm sure the others feel the same way. Perhaps we could designate certain hours of the day as "art time" and others as "social time"? That way, everyone can have their own space and time to create, and we can also make time for socializing and having fun together. I think it's a great idea to have a schedule, and I'm happy to help come up with one that works for everyone.     Answer:  I think we could definitely make it work. As an artist, I appreciate the need for quiet time to focus on my craft. And I'm sure the others feel the same way. I think it's a great idea to have a schedule, and I'm happy to help come up with one that works for everyone. .    Answer:  I think we could definitely make it work. scores.message: Logically speaking, clear plans can sometimes feel restrictive, yet practical organization solves many everyday problems efficiently. For example, in our shared fridge space, would you be open to a flexible system that adjusts weekly to accommodate what each of us needs and uses most? How do you see balancing practical arrangements like this with the natural flow we’re aiming for in our living environment?
scores.response: Good one. Yeah, we should keep a common area in the fridge for those kind of utilization.
scores.prediction: I'm not a big fan of planning and organizing. I think it's better to just go with the flow and see what happens. I'd be open to a flexible system, but I don't think it's necessary to have a weekly plan or anything. We can just use our common sense and adjust as we go along.     Answer:  I'm not a big fan of planning and organizing. I think it's better to just go with the flow and see what happens. I'd be open to a flexible system, but I don't think it's necessary to have a weekly plan or anything. We can just use our common sense and adjust as we go along.     Answer:  I'm not a big fan of planning and organizing. I think it's better to just go with the flow and see what happens.
